message:
feat(widgets): use ART for command palette prefix filtering

Wire the existing AdaptiveRadixTree into the CommandPalette Prefix filter path so prefix-only searches score only indexed title candidates while leaving the default fuzzy search path unchanged.

Add regression coverage for candidate pruning, duplicate titles, action replacement, and cache isolation between Prefix and All match modes.

Closes bd-1cgh9.

Commands:

- cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test visual_effects_pty vfx_golden_hash_registry -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test visual_effects_pty pty_vfx_harness_deterministic_hashes -- --nocapture

Perf guard results (seed=0, tick=16ms, frames=120):
120x40 total_ms_p95 (baseline -> now, Δ):
- metaballs: 3.609 -> 2.849 (-21.06%)
- plasma:    2.649 -> 2.221 (-16.16%)
- doom-e1m1:  1.666 -> 0.732 (-56.06%)
- quake-e1m1: 5.553 -> 1.885 (-66.05%)
- mandelbrot: 6.946 -> 3.672 (-47.14%)

Golden hash registry: PASS (metaballs/plasma/doom-e1m1/quake-e1m1/mandelbrot @120x40, seed=42, 6 frames)
Deterministic hash check: PASS (metaballs @120x40, seed=42, 6 frames)

Conclusion: ≥30% p95 total reduction achieved for at least two heavy effects (doom-e1m1 + quake-e1m1), so bd-3e1t.5.3 success target looks met. Remaining doc refresh work is tracked in bd-3e1t.5.3.15.","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:47:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1cgh9","title":"task(graveyard-§7.5): ART (Adaptive Radix Tree) for command palette search","description":"Use an Adaptive Radix Tree (ART) for the command palette prefix search, providing O(k) lookup where k = key length, independent of dataset size. ART adapts node size to key distribution, achieving near-optimal memory usage.

## Graveyard Entry

Section 7.5 -- Adaptive Radix Tree
Priority: 3

## Background and Math Foundations

ART (Leis et al. 2013, The Adaptive Radix Tree: ARTful Indexing for Main-Memory Databases) is a radix tree with adaptive node sizes:
- Node4: 4 keys, 4 children (for sparse nodes)
- Node16: 16 keys, 16 children (SIMD-searchable)
- Node48: 256-entry index array + 48 children (for denser nodes)
- Node256: 256 children (for very dense nodes)

Performance characteristics:
- Lookup: O(k) where k = key length (NOT dependent on n = number of keys)
- Prefix scan: O(k + m) where m = number of matches (ideal for autocomplete)
- Memory: adapts to key distribution (sparse keys use small nodes)
- Cache performance: nodes fit in cache lines (Node4 = 32 bytes, Node16 = 128 bytes)

For command palette:
- Commands are strings (e.g., file:open, edit:undo, view:toggle-sidebar)
- User types prefix, ART returns all matching commands
- With 1000+ commands, ART provides instant results vs O(n) linear scan
- Fuzzy matching: extend with edit-distance bounded search (Damerau-Levenshtein)

## Structured Logging

All tests and benchmarks must emit structured JSONL evidence:
- op: art_insert, key, node_type_before, node_type_after, depth, timestamp_ns
- op: art_lookup, prefix, match_count, scan_time_ns, node_visits
- op: art_benchmark, command_count, art_time_ns, btree_time_ns, linear_time_ns, speedup
- op: art_memory, command_count, art_bytes, btree_bytes, node_type_distribution

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ART implementation with insert, lookup, prefix_scan, delete
2. Adaptive node types (Node4, Node16, Node48, Node256) with automatic promotion/demotion
3. Prefix scan returns results in sorted order (for display)
4. Benchmark: ART vs BTreeMap vs linear scan at 100, 1K, 10K commands
5. Integration with command palette widget
6. Optional: fuzzy matching with bounded edit distance
7. All tests emit structured JSONL logs for evidence-ledger integration
8. CI artifacts: lookup latency comparison, memory usage report, node distribution","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:53.779268148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:16:25.507245285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive-radix","alien-cs","art","command-palette","graveyard","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgh9","depends_on_id":"bd-14k2m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cgh9","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1csc","title":"Drag-and-Drop Framework","description":"## Background

Terminal DnD enables:
- File browser file moves
- List item reordering
- Panel reorganization
- Kanban-style boards

Builds on Semantic Events (#1) for drag detection.

## Solution: DnD System

```rust
/// Data being dragged
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DragData {
    pub kind: String,  // e.g., 'file', 'list-item'
    pub payload: Box<dyn Any + Send>,
    pub source_id: Option<WidgetId>,
}

/// Trait for draggable widgets
pub trait Draggable {
    fn drag_data(&self) -> DragData;
    fn drag_preview(&self) -> Option<DragPreview>;
    fn on_drag_start(&mut self) {}
    fn on_drag_end(&mut self, success: bool) {}
}

/// Trait for drop targets
pub trait DropTarget {
    fn can_accept(&self, data: &DragData) -> DropEffect;
    fn on_drag_enter(&mut self, data: &DragData) {}
    fn on_drag_leave(&mut self) {}
    fn on_drop(&mut self, data: DragData, pos: Position) -> bool;
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum DropEffect {
    None,
    Move,
    Copy,
    Link,
}

/// Visual feedback during drag
pub struct DragPreview {
    widget: Box<dyn Widget>,
    offset: (i16, i16),
    opacity: f32,
}

/// Manages active drag operation
pub struct DragManager {
    active: Option<ActiveDrag>,
    targets: Vec<(Rect, Box<dyn DropTarget>)>,
}

impl DragManager {
    pub fn start_drag(&mut self, data: DragData, preview: Option<DragPreview>);
    pub fn update_position(&mut self, pos: Position);
    pub fn drop(&mut self) -> bool;
    pub fn cancel(&mut self);
    pub fn render_preview(&self, frame: &mut Frame);
}
```

## Visual Feedback

```
  ┌── file.txt ──┐  <- Drag preview at cursor
  │    📄        │
  └──────────────┘
  
  [Drop here]  <- Highlighted drop zone
```

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-core/src/dnd.rs (types)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/drag_manager.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/dnd_support.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Drag-and-Drop Framework
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1csc.1","title":"Drag Source Protocol","description":"Enable widgets to be drag sources.

## Requirements
- Draggable trait for source widgets
- Drag data payload (type + content)
- Drag start gesture detection
- Visual feedback during drag

## Proposed API

```rust
/// Trait for widgets that can be dragged
pub trait Draggable {
    /// MIME-like type identifier for the dragged data
    fn drag_type(&self) -> &str;
    
    /// Serialize drag data for transfer
    fn drag_data(&self) -> DragPayload;
    
    /// Optional custom preview widget
    fn drag_preview(&self) -> Option<Box<dyn Widget>> { None }
    
    /// Called when drag starts
    fn on_drag_start(&mut self) {}
    
    /// Called when drag ends (success or cancel)
    fn on_drag_end(&mut self, success: bool) {}
}

/// Payload carried during drag operation
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct DragPayload {
    pub drag_type: String,
    pub data: Vec<u8>,
    pub display_text: Option<String>,
}

impl DragPayload {
    pub fn new<T: Serialize>(drag_type: &str, data: &T) -> Self;
    pub fn text(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    pub fn decode<T: DeserializeOwned>(&self) -> Option<T>;
}

/// Runtime drag state
pub struct DragState {
    pub source_id: WidgetId,
    pub payload: DragPayload,
    pub start_pos: Position,
    pub current_pos: Position,
    pub preview: Option<Box<dyn Widget>>,
}

/// Drag gesture detection thresholds
pub struct DragConfig {
    pub threshold_pixels: u16,      // Min movement to start drag (default: 3)
    pub start_delay_ms: u64,        // Delay before drag starts (default: 0)
    pub cancel_on_escape: bool,     // Esc cancels drag (default: true)
}
```

## Integration with Semantic Events

Uses GestureRecognizer for drag detection:
```rust
// In SemanticEvent enum
DragStart { pos: Position, source: WidgetId },
DragMove { from: Position, to: Position, delta: (i16, i16) },
DragEnd { pos: Position, cancelled: bool },
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/drag/source.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_drag_payload_serialize: Round-trip serialization
- test_drag_threshold: Movement below threshold ignored
- test_drag_cancel_escape: Esc key cancels
- test_drag_preview_custom: Custom preview renders
- test_drag_callbacks: on_drag_start/end called correctly

## Snapshot Tests
- drag_preview_default_80x24
- drag_preview_custom_80x24

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Drag start/move/end semantic events are emitted deterministically given an input event stream.
- [ ] Widget API is ergonomic: a widget can opt into dragging with minimal boilerplate.
- [ ] Unit + snapshot tests pass and have high-signal failure output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:51:18.606985647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:30:13.896641138Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:30:13.896619017Z","close_reason":"Already completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1csc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1csc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
